questions.prompt: Default attribution window in 2026 and why?

choices.text: 7-day click + 1-day view. Shortened after iOS 14.5 ATT because iOS users' attribution signal degrades quickly past 24 hours and Meta needed a defensible default. Click attribution is the dominant signal — a verified action linking the user to the ad. View-through is real but inflates easily, especially for users who would have converted anyway. Read 7-day click for daily decisions; check 28-day click monthly if sales cycle is long.

questions.explanation: 7-day click + 1-day view is the default. Click is the load-bearing signal.

questions.prompt: Meta reports $100k revenue, Shopify shows $50k. What's going on?

choices.text: Different attribution rules. Meta credits every conversion within 7-click + 1-view of an ad interaction, including users who would have bought anyway (last-touch + view-through). Shopify just reports total orders. Meta also includes modeled conversions for iOS-blocked events. Other channels' contribution gets diluted in Meta's view. Reasonable practice: pick e-commerce platform revenue as source of truth, use Meta as a diagnostic, and accept that Meta typically overstates its contribution by 10-30%. Periodic incrementality tests ground the real causal lift.

questions.explanation: Meta's attribution overstates vs platform actual. Pick one source of truth, reconcile.
